{"credits": [], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": 2.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 2.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-295", "name": "Improper Certificate Validation"}], "description": "### Description

A regression was introduced in OpenSearch 2.18.0 that caused the `plugins.security.ssl.transport.enforce_hostname_verification` setting to be ineffective. When this setting was enabled, OpenSearch did not verify that the hostname in a connecting node's TLS certificate matched the hostname of the connection. This could allow a node with a valid certificate (signed by the cluster's trusted CA) but an incorrect hostname SAN to join the cluster.

### Impact

Clusters running affected versions with hostname verification enabled did not receive the expected protection from this setting. A node presenting a certificate signed by the cluster's trusted CA could join the cluster regardless of whether its hostname SAN matched. This regression does not affect certificate validation itself — only the additional hostname verification check.

### Patches

This issue is fixed in OpenSearch 2.19.4 and 3.3.0.

### Workarounds

Use more restrictive values for `plugins.security.nodes_dn` to limit which certificates are accepted for node-to-node communication.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-x5hg-x4gv-j98m", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-07T00:09:18Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x5hg-x4gv-j98m", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-x5hg-x4gv-j98m"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-07T00:09:18Z", "references": ["https://github.com/opensearch-project/security/security/advisories/GHSA-x5hg-x4gv-j98m", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x5hg-x4gv-j98m"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/opensearch-project/security/security-advisories/GHSA-x5hg-x4gv-j98m", "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/opensearch-project/security", "summary": "OpenSearch has ineffective TLS certificate hostname verification", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-07T00:09:21Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-x5hg-x4gv-j98m", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.19.4.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.opensearch.plugin:opensearch-security"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.18.0, < 2.19.4.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "3.3.0.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.opensearch.plugin:opensearch-security"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.0.0, < 3.3.0.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: OpenSearch has ineffective TLS certificate hostname verification
